comment: Bidfood (Bidfood Direct) static snapshot register, RATIOS ONLY. Bidfood's ordering portal is account-gated and its trading terms (clause 4, v. October 2025) treat specific pricing as confidential, so this public register carries no prices: only the plant/animal price ratio per pair, from a hand-curated snapshot taken via a trade account on the snapshot date (ex-VAT list prices as shown to a new account; a contract caterer's negotiated prices can be lower). The price-bearing source register and full harvest live in the private procurement project, where each price was verified against the portal UI. Rendered by build_page.mjs as its own table; never touched by run_scrape_wholesale.mjs and never merged into history_wholesale.json. Matching follows the register discipline of pairs_wholesale.json. Same-brand sweep completed with a second portal session on the snapshot date: pairs carrying same_brand=true compare a brand's own plant-based line against its animal-product line. Ruled out: Richmond (Bidfood lists only the Meat Free line, no Richmond pork sausages), Pukka (no Pukka products at Bidfood), and every Everyday Favourites own-brand pairing (EF pork sausages and EF Vegetable Burger publish piece counts, '1-80pk', with no per-piece weight, so no per-kg basis exists; the EF burger lines also mismatch tiers, spicy bean vs 90% premium gourmet beef).
price_basis: ratios of ex-VAT list prices, new trade account, Bidfood Direct portal (specific prices withheld per Bidfood trading terms clause 4)

meat.label: Everyday Favourites Frozen Minced Beef IQF 90VL 2.5kg

pairs.note: Both frozen, own/value tier (mirrors the jj-mince pairing). The beef side is 90VL (about 10% fat): Bidfood lists no 20% fat mince, the retail comparator tier, and leaner mince costs more, which flatters the plant side. Branded alternative on file: Meatless Farm plant-based mince. Quorn mince excluded as a bonus-pack promotion (1kg + 50% extra free).

Branded plant sausage vs standard fresh pork 8s. Format mismatch flagged: the Quorn side is frozen, the pork side chilled (Bidfood's own-label Everyday Favourites pork 8s pack, 1x80pk, publishes no per-sausage weight, so it could not be used on a per-kg basis).
Comparable patty sizes (109g vs 113g), both frozen catering burgers. The beef side declares 70% beef content and comes in a much larger case (5.4kg vs 2kg; bulk pricing flatters it).

Cheapest per-litre oat line vs cheapest per-litre cow-milk line at the snapshot. Basis mismatch flagged: the oat side is long-life barista formulation in a 6-pack, the milk side fresh in a single 2L bottle.

Same brand and same 5L pack size both sides (the Bidfood twin of the Brakes Hellmann's pair). The non-vegan side is the standard Hellmann's mayonnaise line; Bidfood also lists Light and Professional variants at other prices.

Same brand both sides (mirrors the JJ Heinz pair) but pack sizes differ (5L vs 10L; larger packs are usually cheaper per litre, which inflates the vegan side's per-litre figure). Bidfood lists no 5L Heinz Original; the 5L Heinz Professional is a different recipe tier.
Same brand, sliced format both sides (the Bidfood twin of the Brakes Applewood pair). Pack formats differ (12x200g case vs a single 750g pack).

Same brand and same variant (almond), 20-stick catering boxes both sides (the Bidfood twin of the Brakes Magnum pair; the vegan stick is 85ml here vs 90ml at Brakes, so per-100ml is the comparable basis).
